fact: February 25 is exactly 56 days into the year - that's 8 perfect weeks!
In the Northern Hemisphere, tree sap typically starts flowing faster around this time, making maple syrup season begin in earnest.
February 25 falls during peak cat mating season when outdoor cats often yowl all night long.
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around February 25 in most places, adding up to 14 extra minutes of daylight per week.
February 25 always falls under the Pisces zodiac sign - the fish constellation.
In Antarctica, February 25 marks the end of their brief summer when temperatures start dropping from freezing cold back to absolutely brutal.
Earthworms often start wiggling closer to the surface around this date as soil temperatures rise just enough to wake them from winter sluggishness.
February 25 sits exactly 24 days before the spring equinox - close enough that you can almost smell warmer weather coming.
In northern regions, snow that falls on February 25 often creates the perfect sticky texture for building snowmen because temperatures hover right around freezing.
Skunk mating season typically peaks around February 25, which explains why you might smell that stinky spray more often during late February walks.
The moon appears to rise about 50 minutes later each night around February 25, creating dramatic moonrise timing changes throughout the week.
February 25 marks when many hibernating animals like bears often start stirring restlessly in their dens, even though they won't emerge for weeks.
